title: Panchasara Parshwanath temple
tagline: Panchasara Parshwanath temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: Panchasara Parshwanath Temple is a Śvetāmbara Jain temple located in Patan, Gujarat.
overview.description: Panchasara Parshwanath Temple is a Śvetāmbara Jain temple located in Patan, Gujarat. The temple was constructed in 8th century during the reign of Vanaraja Chavda of Chavda dynasty.

topPlaces.name: Patan, Gujarat
topPlaces.description: Patan () is a city and headquarters of Patan district in the Indian state of Gujarat. Patan was the capital of Gujarat's Chavda and Chaulukya dynasties in medieval times.

topPlaces.name: Rani ki Vav
topPlaces.description: Rani ki Vav (lit. 'The Queen's Stepwell') is a stepwell situated in the town of Patan in Gujarat, India. It is located on the banks of the Saraswati River.

topPlaces.name: Sahasralinga Tank
topPlaces.description: Sahasralinga Tank or Sahasralinga Talav is a now-defunct medieval artificial water tank in Patan, Gujarat, India that was commissioned during the Chaulukya (Solanki) dynasty. It is a Monument of National Importance protected by the Archaeological Survey of India (N-GJ-161).

topPlaces.name: Odhava
topPlaces.description: Odhava is a small village located in Patan Taluka in Northern district of Patan, Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Charup
topPlaces.description: Charup is a village in Saraswati Taluka of Patan district of Gujarat state of India.

items.note: Patan () is a city and headquarters of Patan district in the Indian state of Gujarat. Patan was the capital of Gujarat's Chavda and Chaulukya dynasties in medieval times.
Rani ki Vav (lit. 'The Queen's Stepwell') is a stepwell situated in the town of Patan in Gujarat, India. It is located on the banks of the Saraswati River.

Sahasralinga Tank or Sahasralinga Talav is a now-defunct medieval artificial water tank in Patan, Gujarat, India that was commissioned during the Chaulukya (Solanki) dynasty. It is a Monument of National Importance protected by the Archaeological Survey of India (N-GJ-161).
Odhava is a small village located in Patan Taluka in Northern district of Patan, Gujarat, India.
Charup is a village in Saraswati Taluka of Patan district of Gujarat state of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Panchasara Parshwanath temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Panchasara Parshwanath temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Panchasara Parshwanath temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (101 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (104 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Panchasara Parshwanath temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Panchasara Parshwanath temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Panchasara Parshwanath temple?
faq.a: Stays in Panchasara Parshwanath temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Panchasara Parshwanath temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Patan, Gujarat, Rani ki Vav, Sahasralinga Tank, Odhava, Charup.
